Extensions

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions with SAP Build Best Practices: An Expert Roundtable
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensions and Clean Core with SAP BTP - A Best Practices Conversation

entry.title: IBM Granite.13b.chat Large Language Model- Now GA on the SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content:
SAP’s generative AI Hub is the central place for BTP application developers to access and use generative AI tools and models, and developers can now choose IBM Granite as their large language model (LLM). This article will explain some of Granite’s unique characteristics and then show you how to access and use it.

IBM Granite.13b.chat is a decoder, English-only, enterprise-grade LLM and part of a series of IBM foundation models for generative artificial intelligence tasks. IBM stands behind Granite LLMs with IP indemnification protection according to its Standard Terms and Conditions, to provide users with peace of mind. Granite.13b.chat was trained on enterprise-relevant content that meets rigorous data governance, regulatory, and risk criteria defined and enforced by the IBM AI Ethics Code and Chief Privacy Office. You can read the details here: https://www.ibm.com/downloads/cas/X9W4O6BM. Granite.13b.chat delivers high accuracy for retrieval augmented generation (RAG), summarization, content generation, named entity recognition, insight extraction, and classification.

A considerable portion of Granite training data is from business domains. For business-specific tasks in domains such as finance, some IBM performance benchmarks suggest that the accuracy of Granite models is on par with larger models (70B parameter+) at lower latency and, due to its smaller size (13B parameter), lower infrastructure spending.

AI capabilities in SAP AI Core & SAP AI Launchpad that utilize the generative AI hub can leverage Granite.13b.chat.

Get started by:

  1. Learning how to use foundational models on SAP AI Core (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/ai-core-consumption-llm.html), and
  2. learning how to prompt Granite.13b.chat (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/docs/content/wsj/analyze-data/fm-models-ibm-chat.html?context=wx&audience=wdp).

For more information about Granite.13b.chat and instruction-tuning details, see the model card (https://dataplatform.cloud.ibm.com/login?context=wx&sign_in_target=%2Fwx%2Fsamples%2Fmodels%2Fibm%2Fgranite-13b-chat-v2%3Fcontext%3Dwx%3Fcontext%3Dwx%26audience%3Dwdp).

entry.title: SAP BTP: The Foundation for Intelligent Enterprise with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The digital revolution is in full swing, and at its core lies the transformative power of artificial intelligence (AI). For the tech-savvy enterprise, AI is not just a buzzword, but a strategic imperative. SAP Business AI emerges as a key enabler of this transformation, offering a robust suite of tools that empower businesses to optimize operations, automate processes, and gain a competitive edge.

SAP: A Legacy of Innovation in the Enterprise Landscape

Since its inception in 1972, SAP has been a pioneer in enterprise technology, guiding businesses through technological shifts and helping them leverage new opportunities. With SAP Business AI, SAP continue this tradition, delivering a cutting-edge platform that harnesses the power of AI to redefine business processes.

SAP Business AI: More Than Just Automation

SAP Business AI represents a paradigm shift in how businesses leverage data and technology. It's not just about automating tasks; it's about fundamentally changing how businesses operate and innovate. Think of it as an intelligent layer embedded within your existing SAP landscape, constantly learning and adapting to optimize processes and drive better outcomes.

Key Components and Applications: A Technical Perspective

SAP Business AI is deeply integrated across SAP's entire product portfolio, providing seamless access to AI capabilities within the context of everyday business processes. Here's a closer look at the technical components that make it so powerful:

Across key areas like Finance, Supply Chain, HR, Procurement, Marketing & Commerce, Sales & Services, and IT. This broad reach allows businesses to leverage AI in every aspect of their operations.

SAP Business AI provides a dual approach to AI, offering both pre-built AI services and the tools to build custom AI solutions. For specific business processes, SAP offers ready-to-use AI services that can be easily adapted to unique workflows. These services cover areas such as document processing, data attribute recommendations, and Robotic Process Automation.

At the heart of SAP Business AI lies a robust data foundation. This foundation includes SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as the underlying infrastructure, along with services like SAP Datasphere, Analytics Cloud, and Integration Suite. These tools make it easy to connect, model, and analyze data across different applications, ensuring that AI algorithms have access to the high-quality data they need.

SAP also recognizes the importance of collaboration in the AI ecosystem. Establishing strategic partnerships with other industry leaders, such as Microsoft, Google, AWS, and IBM, to leverage their expertise and technologies. This collaborative approach ensures that SAP Business AI remains at the forefront of AI innovation, so that with leading AI vendors and large language model (LLM) providers, giving customers access to the latest innovations and ensuring flexibility in their AI deployments.

SAP Business AI Approach: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible

SAP's approach to AI is guided by three core principles, as highlighted in the image: Relevant, Reliable, and Responsible.

Relevant: SAP Business AI is designed to be relevant from day one. Its deep integration with SAP applications ensures that AI capabilities are readily available and easy to use within the context of everyday business processes.

Reliable: SAP has a long-standing reputation for delivering reliable enterprise software, and this commitment extends to SAP Business AI. The platform is built on a foundation of high-quality data, rigorous testing, and robust security measures, ensuring that businesses can trust its accuracy and performance.

Responsible: SAP recognizes the ethical implications of AI and is committed to developing and deploying AI in a responsible manner. This includes addressing concerns around bias, discrimination, and privacy, and ensuring that AI is used to augment human capabilities, not replace them.

Assessing Business AI Infused Across the SAP Product Portfolio

AI is already deeply ingrained in SAP's enterprise applications and business technology, used by tens of thousands of their cloud customers. AI-powered capabilities, based on proprietary deep learning and machine learning algorithms, are driving significant business value across SAP's cloud solutions. These capabilities include intelligent document processing, personalized recommendations, natural language processing, forecasting, and more. This means that businesses can leverage AI capabilities within their existing SAP solutions, without having to implement separate AI tools or platforms.

Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

Transforming Business Applications with Generative AI

Generative AI is ushering in a new era of possibilities for business software. It enables the creation of new content, summarization of complex information, automated code writing, and much more. This new generation of AI can reason through business problems and offer solutions that were previously unimaginable.
SAP customers can expect to see tangible value from generative AI use cases across the entire SAP portfolio: Generative AI at SAP 

 Let's explore how generative AI is transforming key areas within the SAP ecosystem:

  • Cloud ERP: Streamlining processes like the verification of delivery notes, automating document processing with greater accuracy and efficiency. For example, imagine automatically processing thousands of invoices with minimal human intervention, using AI to extract relevant data and match it with purchase orders.
  • Human Capital Management: Freeing up managers' time by automating tasks like job postings, candidate sourcing, and performance reviews, while also reducing bias in these processes. AI can help identify the best candidates for a job by analyzing resumes and social media profiles, personalize onboarding experiences by providing tailored content and recommendations, and even predict employee attrition by identifying patterns in employee data.
  • Spend Management & Business Network: Empowering category managers with the information they need to make strategic procurement decisions, leveraging market dynamics and cost structure insights. AI can analyze spending patterns, identify cost-saving opportunities, and optimize supplier relationships by analyzing market data and predicting price fluctuations.
  • Customer Relationship Management: Elevating customer engagement by providing sales teams with personalized insights into customer preferences and purchase history, leading to tailored interactions and improved customer satisfaction. Imagine AI-powered chatbots that provide instant support and personalized recommendations to customers, improving customer satisfaction and reducing support costs. AI can also help sales teams prioritize leads, personalize marketing campaigns, and identify upselling and cross-selling opportunities.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform: Simplifying application development by enabling AI-powered code generation and test script creation, allowing developers to focus on more complex tasks. This can significantly reduce development time and costs, allowing businesses to innovate faster. Tools like SAP Build Code help automate code generation and testing, reducing development time and costs. For example, a developer could describe a desired functionality in natural language, and SAP Build Code would generate the corresponding code automatically.

Recognizing the Integration of SAP BTP with Business AI

SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) serves as the underlying platform for SAP Business AI, providing the infrastructure and tools needed to build and deploy AI-powered applications.

Here are some build-in components of SAP BTP that are particularly relevant for AI development:

  • SAP Build Code: This tool empowers developers to build applications faster with the help of generative AI. The Joule copilot provides intelligent code suggestions and automates repetitive coding tasks, increasing developer productivity. For example, Joule can automatically generate code for common tasks, such as creating user interfaces or connecting to databases.
  • SAP Build Process Automation: This no-code solution enables business users to automate workflows and processes without writing any code. Its intuitive drag-and-drop interface and AI capabilities make it easy to create and deploy automations. This empowers business users to automate tasks without relying on IT, increasing agility and efficiency.
  • SAP HANA Cloud: This powerful in-memory database is optimized for handling large volumes of data and complex analytical queries, making it ideal for AI applications. Its ability to process data in memory allows for real-time analysis and insights, which is crucial for many AI use cases.
  • SAP Analytics Cloud: This cloud-based analytics platform provides a comprehensive suite of tools for data visualization, analysis, and planning. The integration of Joule, SAP's generative AI copilot, further enhances the platform's capabilities, enabling users to gain deeper insights from their data. For example, users can ask Joule to "show me the top 10 customers by revenue" or "predict next quarter's sales".

Focusing on the future of AI in the enterprise

SAP Business AI is more than just a technology; it's a strategic enabler for the modern enterprise. By seamlessly integrating AI across its product portfolio, SAP is empowering businesses to optimize processes, automate tasks, gain valuable insights, and ultimately achieve better outcomes. With a strong commitment to relevance, reliability, and responsibility, SAP Business AI is paving the way for a future where businesses can harness the full potential of AI in an ethical and sustainable manner.

Ready to delve deeper into the world of SAP Business AI? Here are some resources to get you started:

  • Artificial­ Intelligence­ |­ SAP ­Business ­AI
  • AI Solutions on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • AI Ethics | SAP Global AI Ethics Policy
  • SAP ­Community:­ Artificial­ Intelligence­ and­ Machine­ Learning­ at­ SAP
  • SAP ­Learning:­ Tutorials­ for­ SAP ­Developers­
  • Discover SAP Business AI features
  • Implement your use cases on SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Learning: how to use the SAP AI Core service on SAP Business Technology Platform

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 4 - Integration via Action
entry.content:
In this series of blogs, I will showcase how to integrate SAP AI Core services into SAP Build Apps to develop applications with AI capabilities.

  • Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup
  • Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function
  • Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API
  • Part 4: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action (current blog)

SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Action

Currently, the recommended solution for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the new feature Action Integration. In this way, a SAP Build Action project (https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/create-actions-project ) for SAP AI Core service will be created first, and then integrated into your SAP Build Apps application.

1. Create an action in Lobby. and select "Build from Scratch." 

2. Add a custom action, select the operation as "POST," and provide the value of the endpoint according to your deployment of the AI core service, which you have setup in Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup

3. Add fields to Input Parameter and Body according the requirement of your SAP AI Core service instance

For examples:

sample JSON for input where you can get from https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-ai-core/sap-ai-core-service-guide/prompt-examples 

 

{
"messages": [
	{
		"role": "user",
		"content": "Your task is to generate a short summary of ..."
	}
],
"max_tokens": 100,
"temperature": 0.0,
"frequency_penalty": 0,
"presence_penalty": 0,
"stop": "null"
}

 

4. Test your SAP Build Action project

To test your SAP Build Action project, you need to add the BTP destination you created at Part 1: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Setup to SAP Build Control Tower. Still remember we set

 

sap.applicationdevelopment.actions.enabled = true

 

in Part 1. This property makes the destination discoverable in the SAP Build Control Tower.

To understand more about how to manage destinations in Control Tower, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/manage-destinations

After adding the BTP destination, you can select it in your Action project and test it.

Once the test is successful, you can open the API response body and click "Generate Output" to populate your Action Output structure automatically. For more details, please refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/testing-actions Now, you have completed the creation of an SAP Build Action to consume the SAP AI Core service with just a copy and paste and a few mouse clicks.

5. Release and publish your action

6. Open your SAP Build Apps project, navigate to the Data tab, click "Add Integration," then select the "SAP Build Library" tile, browse, select the action you just published, and enable it in your SAP Build Apps project.

7. Switch back to "UI Canvas" tab, click the UI component, where you can want to trigger the action, open "Logic Canvas", drag and drop "Tigger Action" flow function to logic canvas and configure it

8. Please make sure to specify the correct BTP destination that you used when you created your action.

9. Configure the data binding for input and output of "Trigger Action" flow function

10. Preview and deploy your app on both web and mobile

To summarize what we have done, without writing a single line of code, just copy and paste, drag and drop, and a few mouse clicks, we created an SAP Build Action to consume SAP AI Core services, integrated it into SAP Build Apps, and developed an application with AI capabilities.

Compared to Part 2: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via Javascript function where you need to write JavaScript code, and  Part 3: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services - Integration via REST API where you need to manually configure the resource schema for the REST API, the new solution with Action Integration offers a much better experience.

In future blogs, I will further discuss how to use Action Integration to enable more AI capabilities, such as RAG (Retrieval-augment generation), image analysis, anomaly detection, voice-to-text, etc. I will also write other blogs on how to use Action Integration to perform more complex data transactions, such as OData linking, deep inserts, etc. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Suncor Energy derives business value through SAP Business AI
entry.content:
The SAP MaxAttention CoE NA Team has been recognized as a prestigious SAP Innovation Awards 2024 Finalist! This accolade celebrates our innovative work with SUNCOR ENERGY, leveraging SAP Business AI to drive significant advancements. Our collaboration with Suncor exemplifies the power of cutting-edge technology in transforming business operations. Through SAP Business AI, we have delivered intelligent solutions that enhance efficiency, optimize processes, and foster sustainable growth. CIO Magazine has published an article featuring this success story.

Business Challenges

Suncor Energy has a legal and regulatory obligation to charge tax on intercompany transactions. Currently, the intercompany journal entries do not have tax amounts. The challenge is to compute the tax amount based on tax code and/or tax jurisdiction and post the journal entry with the consolidated tax amount. The solution should reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.

Solution

As part of its monthly closing process, Suncor needed to reconcile inter-company tax postings for journal entries, which involved significant manual effort. To streamline this task, Suncor collaborated with the SAP MaxAttention team to develop an automation solution. Within less than four weeks, a prototype was delivered, followed by a successful production deployment in under six weeks. A key highlight of the solution was its ability to leverage Suncor’s existing SAP investments, enhancing and optimizing the process to meet evolving business challenges with rapid time-to-value. The hyper-automation solution utilized SAP Business AI, SAP Build Process Automation, S/4HANA REST APIs, Fiori UI, and SAP HANA Cloud, resulting in a reduced TCO and improved ROI.

Outcomes: 

  • Transaction Volume – Savings of $1.176 Million Interest and Fines Exposure (annually)
  • 40 pairings with over 5K customer line items per execution
  • ​​The Inter-company Tax Automation project helped Suncor reduce the risks associated with regulatory filing, minimize manual effort, and shorten the period-end closing time.
  • Suncor end users are happy with the solution, and the adoption is very high due to the reduction in tedious manual intervention and data processing; end users are now able to focus on strategic tasks rather than mundane data validation activities.

For further details, please refer to: https://www.cio.com/article/3544650/suncor-energy-derives-business-value-through-business-ai-and-automation.html 

Contact Person:  Rohit Dwivedi, Kiran Kola

entry.title: How Generative AI Can Assist in SAP Fiori Applications: A Practical Demonstration
entry.content:
What if your SAP Fiori applications could anticipate your needs and provide intelligent suggestions to streamline your workflow? With the rapid advancements in artificial intelligence, integrating generative AI models into enterprise applications is no longer a distant future but a present opportunity to enhance efficiency and decision-making.

In this post, I'll share a practical demonstration of how I integrated a generative AI model into an SAP Fiori application using OpenUI5. This solution enhances the creation of maintenance orders by providing AI-generated suggestions based on the order's title and description, helping users avoid overlooking critical steps.

The Solution

I developed a frontend application using OpenUI5, hosted on Vercel, that mirrors a maintenance order request interface in SAP Fiori. The key enhancement is the addition of AI-generated suggestions that appear as the user inputs the maintenance order's title and description.

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

 

 These suggestions offer important steps for performing the task, effectively serving as a smart checklist to assist users in making informed decisions.

Technical Details

Development Environment

  • Frontend: Built with OpenUI5 and deployed on the Vercel platform.
  • Backend: A locally running API utilizing a generative AI model (LLM) to process user inputs and generate suggestions.

Integration with AI

The integration with the generative AI model is achieved through HTTP requests to the local API. When the user enters the maintenance order's title and description, this data is sent to the API, which processes it and returns a list of suggested steps.

 

 

 

 

.route('/infer', methods=['POST'])
def infer():
    """Endpoint para gerar conteúdo com a API do Google."""
    data = request.get_json(force=True)

    if not data or 'prompt' not in data:
        return jsonify({'error': 'No prompt provided'}), 400

    try:
        prompt_parts = [data['prompt']]
        response = model.generate_content(prompt_parts)
        output_text = response.text

    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"Erro na inferência: {e}")
        return jsonify({'error': str(e)}), 500

    return jsonify({'output_text': output_text})

 

 

 

Interface Implementation

The user interface is crafted to be intuitive and familiar for SAP Fiori users, which helps to reduce the learning curve. Standard OpenUI5 components are utilized to gather user input and present AI-generated suggestions.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

<!-- Botão "Gerar Sugestões" -->
                <Button id="generateSuggestionsButton" text="Gerar Sugestões" press="onGenerateSuggestions" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />

                <!-- Lista de Passos Sugeridos -->
                <Label text="Passos Sugeridos" class="sapUiSmallMarginTop" />
                <List id="suggestionsList" width="80%" items="{suggestionsModel>/suggestions}">
                    <StandardListItem title="{suggestionsModel>}" />
                </List>

 

 

 

Demonstration in Action

Here's how the application works in practice:

  1. Title: "Air Filter Replacement"
  2. Description: "The air filter of the compressor needs to be replaced due to particle buildup."

Upon entering this information, the user receives immediate suggestions:

  • 1. Turn off the compressor and disconnect it from the electrical power.
  • 2. Locate the air filter, which is usually in a plastic or metal case.
  • 3. Loosen the screws or clips securing the air filter box.
  • 4. Remove the old air filter and inspect it for dirt and damage.
  • 5. Insert the new air filter into the housing and secure it with the screws or latches.
  • 6. Replace the air filter box on the compressor.
  • 7. Turn on the compressor and check for air leaks.
     
     

You can test the live application yourself here: https://fiori-app-form.vercel.app/ 

This practical demonstration highlights the potential of integrating generative AI models into SAP Fiori applications. While this example operates locally without OData communication, it showcases how AI can be leveraged to improve daily tasks and workflows.

 

I'm excited to hear your thoughts! Share your experiences, ideas, and questions in the comments.

Thank you for reading! If you're interested in more technical details or have any questions, feel free to reach out.

SAP Fiori SAP AI Core SAP AI Launchpad  Artificial Intelligence SAP Maintenance Assistant mobile app

entry.title: SAP Field Service Management 2411 - Available Now!
entry.content:
SAP Field Service Management 2411 builds on our strategic investment in generative AI for field service organizations, now with extended multilingual AI support. In addition, we introduced several new features designed to simplify user workflows, strengthen integration, and increase functionality. In this blog, you can find an overview of the most notable features, along with a demo video showcasing key highlights. For a complete list of all the features in the 2411 release, click here. In this blog, I will highlight the key features that will help enhance how you manage field services, including:

  • Generative AI: Multilingual support (French and German) for the Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering.
  • User Experience enhancements: New Morning Horizon theme, Business Partner enhancements, manage work time in the Time & Material Journal, extended sort and filter capabilities, extended customization capabilities, and more.
  • Service Map: Visualize activity details on hover, and a simplified view of travel time/distance.
  • Integration: Enhanced SAP S/4HANA Service integration, New Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities, Production Order integration, and item-based accounting for an unplanned service.
  • AI-based scheduling improvements: Simulate the effect of adjustments in auto-scheduling policies, and recalculate travel time for bookings.

Note: The Activity Summary, Equipment Insights, and Natural Language Filtering features require a customer to be licensed for SAP AI Units. For more information about licensing, please contact your SAP Account Executive. 

Generative AI

SAP Field Service Management Generative AI services are now available in French and German, enabling more users to access Gen AI features in their native language. In addition to simplifying workflows, generative AI in SAP Field Service Management makes it easier to unlock and benefit from valuable insights that were previously difficult to access. These enhanced workflows and actionable insights provide substantial value to field service organizations. According to SAP customer benchmarking, by utilizing these features, field service organizations can achieve (on average):

  • ⬆️ 12.5% increase in dispatcher productivity.
  • ⬆️ 5% improvement in first-time fix rates.
  • ⬇️ 15% reduction in time to review and plan service requests.

Take a look at the demo video below showcasing our AI highlights in 2411:


AI Assisted Services - Now Available in German and French

AI Assisted Services (German 🇩🇪)

AI Services in German - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary

 
AI Assisted Services (French 🇫🇷) 

AI Services in French - Intelligent Filtering and Activity Summary
➡️ For more information on AI services such as Intelligent Filtering, Equipment Insights, and the Activity Summary, please refer to the 2408 Release Blog. These translations are made possible using SAP Translation Hub.

AI-based Scheduling Improvements:

Simulate the Effect of Adjustments in Custom Auto-scheduling Policies

The new Policy Simulation feature allows users to test scheduling policy changes in a controlled environment, ensuring no impact on live schedules. This enhancement allows users to configure and evaluate adjustments, such as prioritizing skills levels or enforcing objectives, in a secure environment. By simulating outcomes, organizations can safely refine strategies, and ensure alignment with evolving operating conditions. Learn more about this innovation here.

Recalculate Travel Time for Bookings

Thanks to feedback from our Customer Influence Program, accurate travel times are now easier to achieve via automatic travel time recalculation. Travel times are updated automatically when planning or replanning activities, ensuring dispatchers always have precise data. This enhancement helps reduce manual adjustments, minimizes dispatcher confusion, and improves overall scheduling accuracy. Learn more about this feature here.


User Experience & Usability Enhancements

SAP Field Service Management 2411 also features several key user experience and usability highlights, which are summarized below and in the demo video:


Morning Horizon Theme

The new Morning Horizon theme in SAP Field Service Management introduces a fresh, modern design, providing users with a consistent and visually appealing interface. This standard theme enhances the user experience with improved aesthetics, accessibility, and a cohesive look and feel across all SAP applications. Learn more here.

 

SAP Feedback Survey

This provides an easy way to contribute feedback on product functionality, helping SAP Field Service Management refine and enhance its offerings based on real-world usage and customer needs. Learn more about participating here.


Dispatching Board

Configurable Header Information in the Modal UI (preview)

The new header configuration in SAP Field Service Management allows organizations to customize the key details displayed in the Modal UI for Service Calls and Activities via screen configurations. This enhancement makes it easier to display critical information by tailoring screens to meet your company’s unique requirements. Learn more about this feature here.



Manage Approvals for Worktime in the Time and Material Journal

The enhanced work time management capabilities in SAP Field Service Management improve tracking and approval of time entries through the Time and Material (T&M) Journal. This update harmonizes processes in the T&M Journal and improves workflows for work time approvals. Learn more about this feature here.

 
Option to Display the Activity Cancellation Reason 

Users can now benefit from increased transparency for cancelled activities in SAP Field Service Management, with clear visibility into the reasons for activity cancellations. For more information, visit the product documentation.


Service Call Extension Tab in the Modal UI

Extensions in SAP Field Service Management now provide greater flexibility for placement in desired outlets. The new Service Call extension tab in the Modal UI allows users to position extensions in more key locations, ensuring they are easily accessible and aligned with operational priorities. For more details, click here.

Sample Extension

 
Option to Save Column Layouts in all List Views (Preview)

The 2411 release provides enhanced flexibility for customizing column settings and layouts of the activity list. Users can easily configure visible fields and group data to align with specific operational needs and tactical scheduling scenarios, creating a more intuitive user experience with faster access to relevant data. For further info, see here.

 
Enhancements for List Views

This release introduces improved filtering capabilities, offering enhanced options for managing list views. Users can now easily filter by key information when working with a list view in the planning list, making this workflow faster and more efficient. More information, here.


Service Map

Quick Access to Activity Details

Dispatchers can now view essential activity information from the Service Map by simply hovering over an activity, reducing the number of clicks. This popup allows dispatchers to instantly view key details, and can be easily configured to display the companies specific requirements. For more details, please see here.


Service Map – Close/Dock Time-Distance Popup Box

When using the Service Map, dispatchers now can easily view detailed travel information directly on the Service Map with a simple pop-up box. This pop-up displays both travel time and distance, and can be opened, moved, and closed as needed, allowing dispatchers to quickly access and manage information without cluttering the screen. More details here.


Integration Improvements

New Fiori App for Viewing SAP Field Service Management Activities

The new SAP S/4HANA Fiori App for viewing SAP FSM activities provides a simplified and intuitive way to access activity details from SAP Field Service Management. With an organized data presentation, users can quickly navigate through activities and view critical information such as status, technician assignments, and partner data, directly from SAP S/4HANA. Get the full details here.


Cancellation of Activity from SAP S/4HANA

In SAP S/4HANA, SAP FSM activities can now be cancelled with detailed reasons, while seamlessly integrated into SAP Field Service Management. This ensures consistent data and improved transparency across both systems. For more details, refer to the help documentation.


Item-based Accounting for Unplanned Service

Item-based accounting and revenue recognition in SAP S/4HANA Service now integrates with SAP Field Service Management to support unplanned services. This enhancement ensures that revenue is accurately tracked and recognized based on actual service execution, bridging financial, profitability, and operational data for improved transparency and compliance. See the support documentation here.


Integration of Production Orders as Service Calls

Production Order integration with SAP Field Service Management enhances the connection between SAP Field Service Management and SAP S/4HANA or SAP ECC. This integration enables scheduling, execution, and confirmation of production order operations, with support for time effort, materials management, and more. More information here.

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2411 enhances the field service value chain with multilingual generative AI, streamlined workflows, and improved integration capabilities. We invite you to explore these new capabilities, share your feedback, and help us shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. Stay tuned for the 2502 release, where even more innovations are on the way!

Get in touch!

Thoughts or questions? Feel free to share any feedback in a comment. It would also be great to hear directly from you which AI innovations you think could be the most impactful for future releases. For related posts and more information, please join our SAP Field Service Management Community page and explore our SAP Field Service Management Website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2411 Release Demo here, and I encourage you to register for our upcoming webinars for the most up to date information by registering here.
